ogTitle: Chapman Bore vs Charlton Gully: Climate Safety

heading: Chapman Bore vs Charlton Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chapman Bore, South Australia and Charlton Gully, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chapman Bore has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Charlton Gully (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chapman Bore or Charlton Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chapman Bore has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Charlton Gully scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chapman Bore scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Charlton Gully scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chapman Bore or Charlton Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chapman Bore has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
